source.domain: small engines, lawn equipment, generator accessories, carburetor/linkage hardware, and service fixtures

requirements.part_name: Small Engine Flywheel Holding Clamp - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Small Engine Flywheel Holding Clamp'. The broader
use case is: Bench servicing single-cylinder lawn mower, tiller, and pressure-washer engines The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: CNC-machined crescent clamp body with stepped
jaw faces and a threaded locking boss All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks the flywheel rim or cooling
fins in place while the crankshaft nut is loosened or torqued It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires curved gripping geometry, offset bosses, jaw clearances,
internal steps, and load-bearing fillets The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 105 mm x 40 mm x 56 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 9 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=95 mm, Y=20 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 35 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=52 mm, Y=20 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 26 mm x 18 mm x 14 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 9 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 2 mm deep.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M9 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'serrated contact edge' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'side tapped hole' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Small Engine Flywheel Holding Clamp'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
requirements.immutable_requirement_note: After review, this English requirement is the product truth for later SubCAD code generation. Do not simplify it to fit current SubCAD limitations.

source.domain: small engines, lawn equipment, generator accessories, carburetor/linkage hardware, and service fixtures

requirements.part_name: Carburetor Throttle Shaft Alignment Jig - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Carburetor Throttle Shaft Alignment Jig'. The
broader use case is: Repairing float-bowl carburetors used on small engines and generators The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined fixture block with carburetor
mounting holes, shaft-support bores, and datum faces All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds the
carburetor body and throttle shaft square while bushings, plates, or screws are serviced It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines precision coaxial holes,
asymmetric mounting patterns, pockets, reference faces, and small fastener features The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of
round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 72 mm and length 34 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 14 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 24 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 26 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M7 holes through the top flat at X=11 mm and X=22 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.

requirements.part_name: Governor Linkage Calibration Plate - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Governor Linkage Calibration Plate'. The broader
use case is: Setting governor arm and throttle linkage travel on lawn equipment engines The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Flat machined gauge plate with arced slots, dowel-pin
holes, and engraved reference marks All other product elements are external reference items and must
not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Provides fixed reference slots and pin
positions for checking linkage length and lever sweep It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests curved slot layout, datum-based hole placement, thin-plate proportions,
and annotation-friendly mechanical features The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 72 mm and length 111 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 18 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 28 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 103 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=37 mm and X=74 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: opposed wrench flats
functional_features.description: Mill two opposed flats across the outside, leaving 64 mm across flats over the middle third of the length.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 5 mm wide across the top flat at X=55 mm, depth 7 mm.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Portable Generator Exhaust Deflector Bracket - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Portable Generator Exhaust Deflector Bracket'. The
broader use case is: Accessory for redirecting hot exhaust away from generator frames, cables, or
nearby surfaces The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Bent-or-machined angle
bracket with slotted mounting ears, spacer bosses, and shield attachment holes All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Mounts to existing muffler guard points and supports an angled deflector shield It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires angled planes, elongated slots,
standoffs, clearance offsets, and thermal spacing considerations The part is made from 1045 medium-
carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut
the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece.
If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 30 mm x 36 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests circular pockets, radial symmetry with

The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined annular press head with stepped
inner clearance and outer locating lip All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Presses the retaining ring evenly
into a recessed carburetor face without deforming the bulb It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires concentric stepped rings, delicate clearance volumes,
chamfers, wall thickness control, and contact-surface definition The part is made from 1045 medium-
carbon steel, normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or

grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

requirements.part_name: Small Engine Valve Spring Compression Bridge - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Small Engine Valve Spring Compression Bridge'. The
broader use case is: Cylinder-head service on overhead-valve lawn mower and generator engines The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined bridge frame with forked feet,
central threaded boss, and valve-stem clearance window All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Spans the valve
cover area and compresses a valve spring using a screw-driven pad It is intentionally included in
the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines bridge-like structure, threaded boss placement, forked
supports, internal cutouts, and force-path geometry The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 21 mm x 4 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M7 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

requirements.part_name: Fuel Line Quick-Cut Measuring Fixture - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Fuel Line Quick-Cut Measuring Fixture'. The broader
use case is: Preparing replacement fuel hoses for trimmers, blowers, generators, and compact engines
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined channel block with hose grooves,
length stops, and a blade-guide slot All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Locates flexible fuel line at
repeatable lengths and guides a square cut It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Tests semi-cylindrical grooves, adjustable stop pockets, narrow guide slots, clamp
features, and ergonomic fixture geometry The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 115 mm x 70 mm x 24 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 13 mm on the length centerline at X=14 mm and X=101 mm, Y=35 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 38 mm long x 12 mm wide through the part, centered at X=57 mm, Y=35 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 28 mm x 23 mm x 9 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

source.raw_idea_title: Generator Wheel Kit Axle Spacer Block

requirements.part_name: Generator Wheel Kit Axle Spacer Block - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Generator Wheel Kit Axle Spacer Block'. The broader
use case is: Accessory hardware for fitting wheel kits to portable generator tubular frames The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined spacer block with saddle radius,
through-bolt holes, axle bore, and anti-rotation flats All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Maintains axle
spacing and frame clearance while distributing load around the mounting tube It is intentionally
included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires cylindrical saddle cuts, intersecting bores,
frame clearance geometry, flats, and load-bearing transitions The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 53 mm x 18 mm x 16 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

source.raw_idea_title: Air Filter Cover Latch Replacement Bracket

requirements.part_name: Air Filter Cover Latch Replacement Bracket - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Air Filter Cover Latch Replacement Bracket'. The
broader use case is: Repair part for cracked or missing latch mounts on mower and generator air
filter housings The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Small machined bracket
with hooked latch profile, countersunk holes, and raised locating pads All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Provides a rigid replacement latch anchor that screws into existing housing points It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests small-scale functional hooks,
countersinks, raised pads, asymmetric profiles, and tight packaging constraints The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 105 mm x 105 mm x 3 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 13 mm on the length centerline at X=21 mm and X=84 mm, Y=52 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 35 mm long x 7 mm wide through the part, centered at X=52 mm, Y=52 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 26 mm x 35 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 6 mm and undercut 3 mm for registration.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
